_comment: Provenance for the DesignOS material adopted by the design-qa skill (Phase 8). Diff `source.*Rev` and `adopted[].rev` against the live _ds_manifest.json to detect drift. See _kickoff-docs + the Phase 8 work for context.

source.note: DesignOS is a Claude Design project, not a package; this is a local export pulled beside the design-qa-skill repo (DESIGN-CONTEXT.md §0/§6). Re-pull + re-diff before adopting new ids.

applicationLayer.strategy: tokens.css + base.css <link>ed ahead of styles.css; styles.css references DesignOS roles directly (no raw hex, no legacy vars). Dark-only default via data-theme=dark; light theme + tweak axes remain reachable by attribute but no user-facing tweak panel ships.

portableArtifact.strategy: build.mjs concatenates tokens.css + base.css + styles.css into one inlined <style> (same order as the console link chain); mirrors data-theme=dark + the font <link> into the artifact <head>. Portable on file:// — no relative-color, OKLCH only.

browserFixture.surface: capture overlay injected into the live page
browserFixture.strategy: Closed shadow root can't <link>/@import the console stylesheet, so the overlay carries a self-contained, static-OKLCH copy of the dark token set it uses, plus a thin legacy-alias layer (--bg/--text/--border/... → DesignOS roles) so existing rules resolve to tokens. Always dark.

adopted.fidelity: 2 documented deviations (font @import lifted to <head>; 3 relative-color values pre-resolved to static dark --ink OKLCH for file:// portability)

adopted.note: decisions re-expressed in buildless vanilla CSS, not React

adopted.appliedTo: .comment-avatar (inset stroke ring; author-identity bg kept — see deviations)
.marker (console) + .pin / .pin-num (overlay): 50% 50% 50% 2px teardrop, accent-ink glyph, shadow-2; resolved = surface-4 + ink-mid
left Screens sidebar: brand row, file header, search, .eyebrow section + add, rich rows (selected=surface-4), + the row 'action' variant (hover ⋯ → Delete screen)
console shell: NO top bar (actions in panes), three-pane body, identity→left header, Share+presence→right pane; mirrored into artifact/build.mjs
Menu/MenuItem → console/ui/menu.mjs (session FileTrigger menu, screen-row Delete menu, comment ⋯ menu); FileTrigger chevron pattern
Tabs (right-pane Comments|Steps), IconBtn, SectionHeader, AppSwitcher tile (brand mark). Shared file — not a manifest item, no rev.
right Comments pane: in-pane presence+Share action row, search + ⋯ overflow, flat borderless comment cards

the 'Sidebar Steps' composite (components page → Composites): right-pane PILL tabs (active=surface-4) + the Steps TIMELINE (dot/line rail + NN·action stamp + bold target + meta). Not its own manifest item.
comment card anatomy (avatar-left/body-right, crumb/byline/note + foot category tag + ⋯/resolve) on both surfaces

console/ui/icons.mjs inlines the exact lucide glyphs DesignOS maps (search/plus/check/chevDown/comment(MessageSquare)/more(MoreHorizontal)/trash(Trash2)/filter(ListFilter)/sparkles). Offline-safe; no lucide CDN.
overlay static-widget styling (panel, popover, composer pill, recording chip, toast)

adopted.referenceFor: the live capture overlay (DesignOS v2 scope item #3). Overlay REBUILD to this draggable mini-toolbar is PLANNED, not yet built — see designos memory / Phase 9 task.

tokenRoleMap._comment: Legacy design-qa hex vars (formerly in console/styles.css and the overlay shadow root) → DesignOS roles. The console now references roles directly; the overlay keeps these names as an alias layer pointing at the roles.

deviations: console/base.css: font @import moved to HTML <head> (an @import is only honored when it leads a stylesheet; the artifact concatenates tokens+base+styles into one <style>).
console/base.css: the 3 relative-color values oklch(from var(--ink) l c h / A) pre-resolved to static dark --ink oklch(0.86 0.002 250 / A) so the file:// artifact needs no relative-color support.

overlay/inject.js: carries an inlined static-OKLCH copy of the dark token set + a legacy-alias layer (shadow root can't link the stylesheet); the overlay is always dark and does not honor data-theme/tweak axes.

Author-avatar palette (console/ui/comments.mjs) kept as 8 fixed data-identity colors (they encode WHO authored a comment, not chrome) rather than DesignOS's single --grad-avatar; the avatar SHAPE/ring adopts the Avatar atom.
Tweak axes (theme/accent/density/...) are NOT exposed in the QA UI by design (dark-only ship); the token system still supports them by attribute.

driftCheck.how: Re-pull DesignOS context/, then compare: source.tokensRev vs _ds_manifest.json.tokensRev; each adopted[].rev vs the matching _ds_manifest.json item's rev. A changed rev means the upstream atom/foundation moved — re-port and bump this lock.
